reasons: cand_diag cleared its own decision rule: H1 is supported by a real compute-order reorder on GSM8K, H3 is supported by a clear observer-controller gap, and H4 is supported by the HumanEval guard boundary result.

The pilot evidence is mechanism-bearing rather than merely numeric: actual-compute normalization changes headline ordering, code failures remain structurally brittle, and stronger observational signals do not translate into equally strong control policies.
cand_tiger failed its method-forward gate because it ties entropy revision on GSM8K, trails CORE under honest compute accounting, and does not reopen the code story after gating.
cand_minimal and cand_factorization remain plausible backups, but neither is currently better supported by direct pilot evidence than cand_diag.
next_actions: Advance to experiment_cycle with cand_diag as the only active front-runner.
Run full-scale compute-normalized comparisons on reasoning benchmarks and keep actual compute accounting as the primary comparison axis.
Add benefit-bucket and failure-taxonomy analyses so the paper contributes a revision response surface instead of a loose benchmark summary.
Keep code results as boundary evidence unless a later full experiment materially beats Standard on pass@1.
